mention_text: BP announced exit from its 19.75% shareholding in Rosneft resulting in pre-tax charges of approximately $25.5 billion ($24.4B after tax). Board described exiting as a decision taken in the best long-term interests of all our shareholders.
management_action_described: Exit 19.75% Rosneft stake; CEO Bernard Looney and former CEO Bob Dudley resigned from Rosneft board
notes: Announced Feb 27 2022. Q1 2022 replacement-cost loss of ~$20.4B almost entirely from Rosneft write-down. Excluding Russia charge underlying RC profit was ~$6.2B. VERIFIED: $25.5B pre-tax and $24.4B after-tax confirmed. Quote corrected: shareholders not stakeholders.

mention_text: Shell reported $3.9 billion in pre-tax impairment charges (~$3.1B post-tax) related to its Russia exit covering its ~27.5% stake in Sakhalin-2 and other Russian assets. CEO Ben van Beurden: We are shocked by the loss of life in Ukraine which we deplore resulting from a senseless act of military aggression.
management_action_described: Exit Sakhalin-2 LNG stake; exit Nord Stream 2; shut Russian service stations; wind down all Russian operations
notes: Announced Feb 28 2022. $3.9B is PRE-tax (corrected from post-tax). Total Russia charges rose to ~$5B by FY2022. Record Q1 2022 adjusted earnings of $9.13B confirmed. Stock rose 5-8% due to oil price surge. VERIFIED.

mention_text: CEO Chris Kempczinski: The humanitarian crisis caused by the war in Ukraine and the precipitating unpredictable operating environment have led McDonald's to conclude that continued ownership of the business in Russia is no longer tenable. Pre-tax charge of approximately $1.29 billion.
management_action_described: Sold ~850 Russian restaurants to local licensee Alexander Govor; rebranded as Vkusno i Tochka. Retained right to repurchase within 15 years.
notes: Announced May 16 2022; sale closed Jun 12 2022. Russia+Ukraine were ~9% of systemwide revenue (~2% consolidated). Stock resilient -1-2% around announcement as March closure was already priced in. VERIFIED: $1.29B charge date and Kempczinski quote all confirmed.

mention_text: CFO Colette Kress disclosed approximately $400M estimated revenue impact from new export controls restricting sales of A100/H100 and DGX systems to China. Pre-announced via 8-K on August 26 2022 after being notified of forthcoming restrictions.
management_action_described: Developed A800 and H800 compliance chips with reduced interconnect bandwidth designed to meet export control thresholds while still serving China market
notes: BIS rule published Oct 7 2022. NVIDIA pre-announced Aug 26 2022 via 8-K. Stock fell ~3-4% on Aug 26. $400M figure and CFO Kress disclosure confirmed. A800/H800 workaround chips confirmed. VERIFIED.

mention_text: Jensen Huang stated updated export restrictions would cause a significant decline in China sales in Q4 and that NVIDIA expected other markets to different levels make up for some of the shortfall. China had been ~20-25% of Data Center revenue. Analysts estimated $5B+ annualized risk.
management_action_described: Began designing new China-specific chips compliant with updated thresholds; diversified sales to other markets (India Middle East)
notes: Updated BIS rule Oct 17 2023 closing A800/H800 loophole. No single-quarter dollar figure as clean as Round 1. China was 20-25% of DC revenue confirmed. $5B+ annualized risk was analyst consensus (Bernstein Morgan Stanley). VERIFIED.

mention_text: CEO Vincent Clerc stated Red Sea disruption created a significant cost headwind but also tightened capacity supporting freight rates. Cape of Good Hope rerouting added ~10-14 days to Asia-Europe transit. Spot freight rates Shanghai-Rotterdam surged from ~$1500/FEU to ~$4000-5000/FEU by January 2024.
management_action_described: Rerouted all vessels via Cape of Good Hope; paused all Red Sea/Gulf of Aden transits Dec 15 2023; deployed additional vessels to maintain service frequency
notes: Dec 15 2023 was route suspension date (not Hangzhou attack date; Hangzhou attacked Dec 30). Industry-wide ~15-20% capacity reduction. Stock rose 15-20% Dec-Jan. FY2023 revenue ~$47-48B (corrected from $51B which was FY2022). VERIFIED with corrections.

mention_text: Q1 2024 revenue $12.4B above consensus with management attributing beat to Red Sea disruption tightening supply. 2024 guidance upgraded from EBITDA $1-6B to $4-6B explicitly citing Red Sea rerouting as key driver.
management_action_described: Continued Cape of Good Hope routing; leveraged tight capacity for rate increases; deployed surge capacity
notes: Counterintuitive: Red Sea crisis was operationally negative but financially positive for Maersk due to rate surge. Guidance upgrade direction confirmed. VERIFIED.

mention_text: Renault sold 67.69% stake in AvtoVAZ to NAMI (Russian state entity) and 100% of Renault Russia to City of Moscow for symbolic 1 ruble each. EUR 2.3 billion charge booked in H1 2022.
management_action_described: Sold AvtoVAZ and Renault Russia for 1 ruble each; retained 6-year buyback option. CEO Luca de Meo: responsible choice towards 45000 employees while preserving ability to come back.
notes: Announced May 16 2022. EUR 2.3B H1 2022 charge. Buyback option unusual — Renault hedging for potential return. Verify against Renault H1 2022 results (Jul 28 2022).

mention_text: Carlsberg booked DKK 9.8 billion (~EUR 1.3B / ~$1.4B) impairment on Baltika Breweries. Russia subsequently seized the business via presidential decree in July 2023. CEO Jacob Aarup-Andersen called it an expropriation of our assets.
management_action_described: Announced intent to exit March 2022; before sale could complete Russia nationalized Baltika via decree Jul 2023. Carlsberg initiated international arbitration.
notes: Unique case: voluntary exit turned into forced expropriation. DKK 9.8B impairment in 2022; further write-downs in H1 2023 after seizure. Verify against Carlsberg 2022 annual report.

mention_text: Heineken took EUR 300M non-cash impairment charge in H1 2022 for Russia exit. Total cumulative losses approximately EUR 540M including disposal loss. CEO Dolf van den Brink: we do not consider it right to profit from our presence in Russia.
management_action_described: Sold Russian business to Arnest Group for EUR 1 (symbolic). Sale completed Aug 25 2023.
notes: Announced exit Mar 28 2022; sale completed Aug 2023. EUR 300M initial impairment + EUR 240M disposal = EUR 540M total. Verify against Heineken 2022 annual report and H1 2023 results.

mention_text: IKEA suspended Russian operations Mar 3 2022 affecting 17 retail stores 3 manufacturing plants ~15000 employees. Russia and Ukraine together represented ~8-9% of total retail revenue. Write-down approximately EUR 1.5B.
management_action_described: Immediate suspension of all Russian operations; wound down through 2023. Rapidly diversified timber sourcing from Russia (20% of Europe birch/softwood) to Baltic Scandinavian and South American suppliers.
notes: Fastest major corporate exit — 7 days after invasion. Also a procurement_supply_chain impact via timber sourcing. EUR 1.5B write-down (some estimates EUR 1.7B). Verify against Ingka Group 2022 annual summary.

mention_text: Unilever initially continued Russian operations citing obligations to ~3000 employees. Named on Ukraine government sponsors of war list. Faced boycott pressure from B4Ukraine coalition and institutional investors. ESG rating agencies flagged the decision negatively.
management_action_described: Continued supplying everyday essential food and hygiene products but suspended imports/exports media spend and capital investment. Eventually sold Russian ice cream business Jul 2023.
notes: REPUTATIONAL impact case — contrast with IKEA/Shell fast exits. Russia was ~1-2% of group revenue. CEO Alan Jope faced significant backlash. Yale CELI tracker listed Unilever unfavorably. Successor Hein Schumacher accelerated wind-down. Verify against Unilever 2022 annual report.

mention_text: China represented ~29% of ASML net sales in 2023 (~EUR 6.7B of EUR 23.3B). CEO Peter Wennink stated China revenue would drop to roughly 10-15% of sales in 2025. Netherlands formalized export controls on advanced DUV and all EUV systems effective Jan 1 2024.
management_action_described: Complied with Dutch and US export restrictions. ASML had been informally blocked from shipping EUV to China since 2019. Q3 2024 China still ~47% of quarterly revenue due to rush orders before restrictions tightened.
notes: China 29% of sales is massive exposure. Wennink guided 10-15% for 2025 = ~EUR 4B revenue loss. Rush orders inflated 2024 China share temporarily. Verify against ASML Q4 2023 transcript (Jan 24 2024).

mention_text: China including Hong Kong represented ~11-12% of TSMC revenue in 2023. CEO C.C. Wei stated TSMC was fully committed to complying with all applicable rules and regulations. TSMC suspended shipments to certain Chinese clients after Huawei Mate 60 Pro incident Aug 2023.
management_action_described: Halted advanced node (sub-7nm) production for restricted Chinese customers. Lost China demand offset by strong AI-driven demand from NVIDIA Apple AMD.
notes: China 11-12% of revenue — lower exposure than ASML. No specific dollar downside figure disclosed. Compliance-focused messaging. Verify against TSMC Q4 2023 transcript (Jan 18 2024).

mention_text: CEO Lisa Su stated export controls could reduce data center GPU revenue by several hundred million dollars annually. China including Hong Kong represented ~15% of AMD revenue. AMD had been developing China-specific MI250 variant but Oct 2023 rule updates closed the loophole.
management_action_described: Pivoted MI300X sales to non-restricted markets. Su stated demand from non-China markets was significantly oversubscribed partially offsetting China loss.
notes: Several hundred million annually is less precise than NVIDIA $400M but still quantified. 15% China revenue from 2023 10-K. Verify against AMD Q3 2023 transcript (Oct 31 2023).

mention_text: BASF reported high energy costs reduced EBIT by approximately EUR 2.2 billion in 2022 vs 2021. Scaled back ammonia production at Ludwigshafen due to gas prices. Gas costs at Ludwigshafen roughly tripled year-over-year.
management_action_described: Announced permanent downsizing at Ludwigshafen cutting 2600 jobs. Feb 2023 announced EUR 500M annual cost-cutting program. CEO Martin Brudermuller cited European energy costs making the site structurally uncompetitive.
notes: EUR 2.2B EBIT impact is massive and well-documented. Ludwigshafen is world's largest integrated chemical site — energy is primary input cost = procurement_supply_chain. Verify against BASF FY2022 report (Feb 24 2023).

mention_text: Steel Europe segment energy costs rose approximately EUR 1 billion year-over-year. Steel production volumes fell roughly 10-15% in H2 2022. Q4 FY2022 Steel Europe adjusted EBIT dropped to ~EUR 200M from EUR 700M+ in earlier quarters.
management_action_described: Reduced steel output rather than fully idling blast furnaces. Delayed planned Duisburg DRI/hydrogen-based steel transition due to energy cost uncertainty.
notes: EUR 1B energy cost increase on Steel Europe segment. FY ends Sep 30. Verify against ThyssenKrupp FY2022 results (Nov 17 2022).

mention_text: Yara curtailed approximately 3.2 million tonnes of European ammonia production capacity (~35% of European capacity) in Aug 2022. CEO Svein Tore Holsether called the crisis an existential threat to European fertilizer manufacturing.
management_action_described: Curtailed 35% of European ammonia capacity. European delivered cost of ammonia production roughly doubled. Partial restarts as gas prices eased in Q4 2022 but utilization remained ~65-70% through early 2023.
notes: 35% European capacity curtailment is extreme. Q3 2022 EBITDA USD 1.27B still strong due to high fertilizer prices offsetting volume loss. Verify against Yara Q3 2022 report (Oct 21 2022).

mention_text: April 2025 tariffs included rates up to 54% on Chinese imports. Apple stock dropped ~15-20% in days following announcement. Analysts estimated tariffs could add $50-80+ per iPhone if fully applied to China-assembled units. CEO Tim Cook acknowledged tariff headwinds.
management_action_described: Accelerated India manufacturing shift (Foxconn/Tata facilities). China still vast majority of production as of April 2025.
notes: Apple most exposed mega-cap to China tariffs. Q2 FY2025 (May 1 2025) was first call addressing Liberation Day impact. No exact per-unit cost disclosed publicly. Verify against AAPL Q2 FY2025 transcript.

mention_text: Tesla Q1 2025 deliveries fell ~13% YoY. CFO Vaibhav Taneja noted supply chain cost pressures from tariffs on components. China imposed retaliatory tariffs up to 125% on US goods affecting Tesla competitiveness.
management_action_described: Shanghai Gigafactory primarily serves China domestic and Asian/European export markets — limited direct US import exposure. Brand damage from Elon Musk political activities compounded trade friction.
notes: Complex case: delivery decline attributed to mix of tariffs + brand damage + BYD competition. Hard to isolate tariff-only impact = medium confidence. Verify against Tesla Q1 2025 transcript (Apr 22 2025).

mention_text: CEO Doug McMillon and CFO John David Rainey warned tariffs at announced levels could not be fully absorbed and would result in consumer price increases. Walmart sources significant general merchandise from China and other affected countries.
management_action_described: Working with suppliers to mitigate costs and diversify sourcing. Walmart value positioning means consumers trade down to Walmart during inflationary periods — partially offsetting margin pressure.
notes: No specific dollar figure for total tariff cost — described as material to margins. ~60-70% goods sourced domestically but general merchandise heavily China-dependent. Verify against Walmart Q1 FY2026 transcript (May 15 2025).

mention_text: CEO Gary Dickerson stated new export controls would reduce calendar 2023 revenue by approximately $2.5B. China was ~27-33% of revenue pre-controls. Q1 FY2023 guided down to ~$6.7B from prior $6.87B.
management_action_described: Complied with BIS restrictions; curtailed shipments to affected Chinese fabs; continued trailing-edge tool sales
notes: $2.5B annualized CY2023 impact estimate from Q4 FY2022 call (Nov 17 2022). China dropped to ~23% by FY2023 Q2. Verify against AMAT Q4 FY2022 transcript.

mention_text: CEO Tim Archer estimated export controls would reduce CY2023 revenue by $2.0-2.5 billion. China was ~30% of revenue pre-controls. Lam had already begun suspending shipments to affected Chinese customers.
management_action_described: Suspended shipments and support to restricted Chinese customers; continued non-restricted trailing-edge tool sales
notes: $2.0-2.5B estimate from Q1 FY2023 call (Oct 19 2022 — days after BIS rule). China partially recovered to ~26% by Jun 2023 for non-restricted tools. Verify against LRCX Q1 FY2023 transcript.

mention_text: CEO Rick Wallace estimated export controls would reduce FY2023 revenue by $600M-$900M (~6-9% of annual revenue). China was ~29-33% of revenue pre-controls.
management_action_described: Ceased advanced node equipment shipments; continued process control tools for trailing-edge. China stabilized at 22-25% by mid-2023.
notes: $600M-$900M from Q1 FY2023 call (Oct 26 2022). Smallest absolute impact of the 3 equipment makers but still significant. Verify against KLAC Q1 FY2023 transcript.

mention_text: CEO Pat Gelsinger stated updated Oct 2023 controls would have a different but meaningful revenue impact. CFO Dave Zinsner noted China data center revenue had already declined. Estimated low to mid hundreds of millions annually.
management_action_described: Complied with all BIS licensing requirements; lost Huawei as a server chip customer in 2020
notes: China ~27% of Intel revenue ($14.8B of $54.2B in FY2023). Less precise than NVIDIA/AMAT estimates. Verify against Intel Q3 2023 transcript (Oct 26 2023).

mention_text: Huawei was a major customer generating ~$1.8B in a single quarter (Q4 FY2020) after catch-up settlement. Post-ban Huawei revenue dropped to near-zero by FY2022. CFO Akash Palkhiwala noted Huawei was immaterial going forward.
management_action_described: Diversified China customer base to Xiaomi Oppo Vivo who absorbed Huawei share. CEO Cristiano Amon stated other OEMs more than offset the Huawei loss.
notes: Unique recovery story: $1.5-2B annualized Huawei loss largely recovered through other Chinese OEMs. China still ~30% of revenue. Shows geopolitical impact can be offset. Verify against QCOM Q1 FY2022 transcript (Feb 2022).

mention_text: China CAC banned Micron from critical infrastructure purchases May 21 2023. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra stated impact would be low double-digit percentage of total revenue near term. CFO Mark Murphy quantified at-risk amount at roughly $600M-$900M annualized.
management_action_described: Redirected wafer supply to other markets. By Q1 FY2024 Mehrotra confirmed impact at lower end of expectations as global memory demand recovered on AI/HBM boom.
notes: Clean single-event shock. China dropped from ~25% to ~16-17% of revenue. $600-900M at-risk but actual impact at lower end. Verify against Micron Q3 FY2023 transcript (Jun 28 2023).

mention_text: Energy and raw material cost increases amounted to ~EUR 2.8B additional costs in FY2022 vs 2021. EBITDA fell from EUR 3.1B to EUR 1.6B (-48%). CEO Markus Steilemann described an unprecedented cost environment.
management_action_described: Reduced MDI production at Brunsbuttel and TDI output at Dormagen. Utilization fell below 70% in Performance Materials segment.
notes: EUR 2.8B additional energy/raw material costs is massive. EBITDA halved. Verify against Covestro FY2022 annual report.

mention_text: CEO Christian Hartel: We have had to curtail production at sites where energy costs exceed the margin contribution. Energy costs rose EUR 600M YoY. Silicon metal output at Nunchritz cut ~50% (~25kt reduction).
management_action_described: Curtailed 50% silicon metal output at Nunchritz. Permanently closed one electric-arc furnace in early 2023 (~15kt/yr capacity).
notes: Paradoxically overall EBITDA was EUR 1.78B (up YoY) due to strong polysilicon/solar pricing. But silicones segment compressed Q2→Q4. Verify against Wacker Q3 2022 call.

mention_text: Idled 4-5 blast furnaces across Europe (Bremen Germany; Asturias Spain; partial Dunkirk France). European crude steel production cut ~3M tonnes annualized. Energy costs rose ~USD 2B YoY. CEO Aditya Mittal: We continue to flex our production to match market conditions.
management_action_described: Idled blast furnaces rather than operating at a loss; flexed production to match demand. Delayed some decarbonization investments.
notes: Europe EBITDA fell from $8.1B (2021) to $4.9B (2022). H2 2022 only $1.1B vs $3.8B in H1. European shipments Q4 2022 down 17% YoY. Verify against ArcelorMittal Q3 2022 call and 20-F.

mention_text: GM withdrew full-year 2025 guidance citing tariff uncertainty. CFO Paul Jacobson stated 25% tariff on imported vehicles and parts could cost approximately $4-5B annually. GM imports ~850K vehicles/year from Mexico Canada South Korea.
management_action_described: CEO Mary Barra: We are taking decisive actions to mitigate tariff impacts including accelerating US sourcing but the magnitude creates near-term cost pressure. Withdrew guidance.
notes: $4-5B annual exposure is enormous — GM withdrew guidance entirely which is very rare. Verify against GM Q1 2025 transcript (Apr 29 2025).

mention_text: CFO John Lawler: We estimate a roughly $1.5 billion hit to our 2025 adjusted EBIT from tariffs. ~$500M from steel/aluminum (Section 232) plus ~$1B from vehicle/parts tariffs. 30-40% of North American vehicle content crosses international border.
management_action_described: Working on mitigation; announced selective price increases of $600-$2000 on certain models to partially pass through costs.
notes: $1.5B is company-disclosed and specific. Ford imports heavily from Mexico (Maverick Bronco Sport) and Canada. Verify against Ford Q1 2025 transcript (May 5 2025).

mention_text: ~50% of Nike footwear and ~28% of apparel manufactured in Vietnam. Reciprocal tariffs set 46% on Vietnam and 32% on Indonesia. Analysts estimated full tariff regime could add $3-4B in annual import costs (~6-8pp gross margin impact). Nike stock fell ~14% in week after April 2 announcement.
management_action_described: CEO Elliott Hill acknowledged significant uncertainty around trade policy; evaluating supplier diversification and selective pricing. 90-day tariff pause to 10% provided temporary relief.
notes: $3-4B is analyst estimate not company-disclosed = medium confidence. 77% of shoes from Vietnam+Indonesia is massive exposure. Landed cost could rise $8-12/pair. Verify against Nike Q3 FY2025 call (Mar 20 2025) and post-April analyst reports.

mention_text: CFO Adam Karlsson stated freight costs rising due to Houthi attacks forcing Cape of Good Hope rerouting adding ~10-14 days transit from Bangladesh China India to European distribution centers. Spot rates surged to $4000-5000/FEU from $1500. Some spring collection arrival delays.
management_action_described: Long-term freight contracts and inventory buffers limited impact. Flagged freight as headwind to 2024 gross margin improvement but described impact as manageable.
notes: Less severe than feared — H&M had inventory buffers. Full-year 2024 gross margin still improved to 53.3%. Verify against H&M Q1 2024 report (Mar 28 2024).

mention_text: Retail chief Tolga Oncu confirmed Red Sea disruptions causing significant impact on product availability Jan 2024. Shipments rerouted via Cape of Good Hope adding ~2 weeks. Some products faced delays and potential out-of-stocks in European stores.
management_action_described: Absorbed increased freight costs rather than raising prices. Used inventory buffers to manage delays. CEO Jon Abrahamsson Ring described it as a short-term disruption.
notes: IKEA chose to absorb costs not pass through to consumers — margin hit but brand-positive decision. Separate from Russia exit (different event_id). Verify against Ingka Group Jan 2024 statements.

mention_text: Qatar-to-Europe LNG cargoes rerouted via Cape of Good Hope adding ~10-12 days. LNG spot charter rates rose from $40-50K/day to peaks above $80-100K/day in Jan 2024. CEO Patrick Pouyanné noted rerouting tightened LNG shipping market. Q1 2024 Integrated LNG adjusted net income $1.5B.
management_action_described: Benefited from tighter LNG market as one of world's largest LNG traders (~50 Mt/y portfolio). Higher spot prices in Integrated LNG segment.
notes: Another counterintuitive case like Maersk — disruption was financially positive for TotalEnergies. LNG trading profits up. Verify against TotalEnergies Q1 2024 results.

mention_text: Cumulative Ireland DPC GDPR fines ~EUR 2.5B through 2023 including EUR 1.2B for EU-US data transfers (May 2023). Over 40000 people working on safety/security costing ~$5B/year. G&A rose to $4.0B in Q1 2023 from $3.4B YoY driven partly by regulatory compliance.
management_action_described: Built dedicated EU data centers for data residency; hired massive compliance/safety workforce; appealed major fines
notes: EUR 2.5B in fines is exact (sum of DPC decisions). $5B/yr compliance workforce from Zuckerberg Q4 2022 call. Blends content moderation + privacy. Verify against Meta 2022 10-K and Q1 2023 transcript.

mention_text: TikTok spent over $1.5B on Project Texas routing all US user data through Oracle Cloud. Total cost grew to estimated $2B+ by late 2023. Hired ~2500 dedicated US data security employees. CEO Shou Zi Chew testified to Congress Mar 2023.
management_action_described: Project Texas (Oracle data hosting); hired 2500 US Data Security staff; $100-200M/yr in lobbying and legal costs fighting forced-sale legislation
notes: $1.5-2B Project Texas is confirmed. Legal/lobbying costs are estimates. Forced divestiture act signed Apr 2024. Verify against congressional testimony and The Information reporting (Nov 2023).

mention_text: Non-interest expenses related to regulatory compliance rose ~EUR 400M YoY in 2022 partly driven by Russia sanctions implementation. CEO Christian Sewing: We added several hundred compliance FTEs specifically for sanctions-related work.
management_action_described: Added hundreds of compliance FTEs; enhanced sanctions screening systems; wound down ~EUR 1.5B in Russian exposure
notes: EUR 400M incremental compliance cost is directional from annual report not a standalone line item. Verify against Deutsche Bank 2022 Annual Report.

mention_text: Financial crime compliance costs were $3.4B globally for 2022 (up from ~$3.0B in 2021). ~$400M increase attributed partly to Russia sanctions screening. Took $300M in restructuring charges winding down Russia commercial banking.
management_action_described: Wound down Russia commercial banking operations; enhanced global sanctions screening infrastructure
notes: $3.4B total financial crime compliance is from 2022 annual results. Russia attribution is approximate. Verify against HSBC 2022 Annual Report.

mention_text: R&D spending surged from $15.3B (2018) to $23.8B (2023) — ~$8.5B/yr increase directed at replacing US-origin technology. Developed HarmonyOS (Android replacement) with 5000+ developers. HiSilicon shifted chip fabrication to SMIC after TSMC cutoff.
management_action_described: Built alternative technology stack: HarmonyOS HiSilicon chips SMIC fabrication. Revenue fell from $123B (2019) to $92B (2022) before recovering to $99B (2023).
notes: ~$30B cumulative incremental R&D (2019-2023) blends forced compliance with strategic choice. Strongest regulatory_compliance case but also touches innovation_ip. Verify against Huawei Annual Reports 2018-2023.

mention_text: Arm China JV CEO Allen Wu refused removal in Jun 2020 retaining company chop and running JV independently for 2+ years. Arm China accounted for ~25% of Arm revenue (~$670M of $2.68B FY2023). JV developed unauthorized IP products (XPU) outside Arm control.
management_action_described: Negotiated complex shareholder restructuring. Wu finally ousted Apr 2022. IPO prospectus (Aug 2023) listed China JV as material risk factor. Analysts estimated IPO valuation discounted $5-8B.
notes: Arm China = 25% of revenue at risk. IP leakage via unauthorized XPU development. Geopolitical driver = China semiconductor IP localization push. Verify against Arm S-1 (Aug 2023).

mention_text: NIH investigated ~500 scientists for undisclosed foreign ties; >100 removed from NIH-funded positions. Total grants affected exceeded $185M. Presidential Proclamation 10043 (May 2020) denied >3000 student/researcher visas in first 2 years.
management_action_described: MIT ended iFlyTek partnership (Apr 2020). MIT-China program active projects declined ~40% by 2021. Broad chilling effect on US-China academic collaboration.
notes: SECTOR_LEVEL: Use for Model 1 event severity training only. Exclude from Model 2/3 company-specific training. $185M in grants from NIH Office of Extramural Research (Jun 2021). 3000+ visas from State Dept data (Reuters Dec 2021). Not a single company — sector-wide. Verify against NIH data and Reuters reporting.

mention_text: National Farmers Union estimated £60M in crop losses from fruit and vegetables rotting unpicked due to labor shortages. EU freedom of movement ended Jan 1 2021. Some farms lost 20-30% of harvest.
management_action_described: Attempted to recruit domestic workers with limited success. Seasonal Workers Pilot Scheme expanded but insufficient.
notes: SECTOR_LEVEL: Use for Model 1 event severity training only. Exclude from Model 2/3 company-specific training. £60M crop losses from NFU Sept-Oct 2021 estimate. EU nurse registrations fell 87% (9400 to 1200). Sector-wide not single company. Verify against NFU data and Nuffield Trust (Dec 2021).

mention_text: H-1B denial rates surged from ~6% (FY2015) to ~24% (FY2020). Infosys responded by hiring ~10000 US workers and opening US innovation hubs at significantly higher cost. Estimated $200-300M/yr added labor cost.
management_action_described: Hired 10000 US workers; opened US innovation hubs. US median software engineer salary $110-130K vs H-1B roles at $80-90K = ~$30-40K premium per worker.
notes: $200-300M/yr incremental from Jefferies 2019 analyst estimate. Infosys denial rates >30% for initial petitions FY2019. Verify against USCIS data (NFAP/Stuart Anderson) and Infosys annual reports.

mention_text: DarkSide ransomware group (Russia-linked) encrypted IT systems May 7 2021. Paid $4.4M ransom in Bitcoin (DOJ recovered $2.3M in June). Pipeline shut ~6 days carrying ~45% of US East Coast fuel supply. CEO Joseph Blount: hardest decision in 39 years.
management_action_described: Paid ransom; shut pipeline May 7-12; DOJ recovered 63.7 BTC. Triggered emergency declaration in 17 states + DC. National gas prices spiked to $3.04/gal.
notes: SECTOR_LEVEL: Use for Model 1 event severity training only. Exclude from Model 2/3 company-specific training. $4.4M ransom confirmed by CEO testimony (Jun 8 2021). ~$40-50M estimated revenue loss from 6-day shutdown. Net ransom cost ~$2.1M after DOJ recovery. Verify against Blount congressional testimony.

mention_text: Russian SVR/APT29 compromised Orion software update affecting ~18000 customers. SolarWinds reported $40M in direct incident costs through 2021. Stock dropped ~40% after Dec 13 2020 disclosure. Revenue fell from $1.02B (2020) to $981M (2021).
management_action_described: Deployed massive remediation effort. Rebuilt Orion platform. Microsoft deployed >500 engineers to investigate. FireEye released countermeasures for stolen Red Team tools.
notes: $40M direct costs from SolarWinds 10-K filings. Microsoft 500+ engineers from Brad Smith blog (Feb 2021). US govt remediation $100M+. Verify against SolarWinds 2021 10-K.

mention_text: Turkish lira lost ~40% vs USD Jan-Aug 2018. Coca-Cola disclosed 4-percentage-point currency headwind on Q3 2018 net revenue. CEO James Quincey: Currency was a significant headwind particularly from the Turkish lira and Argentine peso. FY2018 had 8% unfavorable currency impact on comparable EPS.
management_action_described: Hedging programs; pricing adjustments in local currency to partially offset. Eurasia/MEA segment reported revenue decline 9% in Q4 2021 during second lira crisis.
notes: 4pp Q3 2018 currency headwind and 8% FY2018 EPS impact are from earnings materials. Lira crisis recurred late 2021. Verify against KO Q3 2018 transcript (Oct 30 2018).

mention_text: Milei government devalued peso 54% on Dec 12 2023 (366 to 800 ARS/USD). Hyperinflation accounting under IAS 29 resulted in ~EUR 600M negative impact on Unilever reported turnover. Total FY2023 negative currency translation impact EUR 2.3B with Argentina as largest contributor.
management_action_described: Applied hyperinflation accounting (IAS 29) for Argentine subsidiary. CEO Hein Schumacher noted Argentina was the most significant single-country currency headwind.
notes: EUR 600M Argentina FX impact and EUR 2.3B total from Unilever FY2023 results (Feb 8 2024). Note: this is the same Unilever that had Russia reputational issues — different event. Verify against Unilever FY2023 annual report.

mention_text: After Feb 2022 sanctions Citigroup disclosed ~$9.8B total Russia exposure. By Q4 2022 CEO Jane Fraser stated ~$7.8B remained trapped in Russia. By Q2 2023 reduced to ~$5.4B but still ~$2.4B in local profits and capital unable to be repatriated. Took $170M reserve build.
management_action_described: Unable to wind down Russian subsidiary AO Citibank. Reduced exposure from $9.8B to $5.4B. Ongoing restrictions on dividend payments and capital repatriation.
notes: $9.8B initial exposure and $7.8B trapped capital from Citi earnings and 10-K filings. $2.4B still unrepatriatable as of Q2 2023. Verify against Citi Q4 2022 transcript (Jan 2023) and 2023 10-K.

mention_text: SocGen sold Rosbank to Interros Capital (Vladimir Potanin) for a fraction of book value.
